title: Audit current machine
description: Record OS, shell, package managers, Git config, SSH keys, editor, terminal app, and language runtimes.

title: Create install manifest
description: Turn baseline dependencies into a maintained Brewfile and document manual installers.

title: Track shell dotfiles
description: Move reusable zsh config, aliases, functions, and PATH rules into tracked dotfiles.

title: Track Git configuration
description: Track reusable Git defaults, global ignore rules, signing notes, and commit conventions.

title: Create dotfile linking flow
description: Verify that scripts/link_dotfiles.sh safely backs up existing files and links tracked config.

title: Create secrets policy
description: Document what never goes in Git and create examples for local env files.

title: Configure language runtimes
description: Choose and document runtime managers for Node, Python, Go, Rust, or other stacks used during the project.

title: Create reusable terminal profile notes
description: Document terminal app settings, fonts, shell theme, keybindings, and color choices.

title: Create new-machine bootstrap test
description: Test clone plus bootstrap on a clean user account, VM, or fresh directory simulation.

title: Add environment verification
description: Expand scripts/verify_environment.sh so it reports missing commands and broken links.

title: Document recovery workflow
description: Write how to restore the environment after a laptop replacement or clean install.

title: Final portability review
description: Confirm the repo can bootstrap the environment and that unfinished tools have follow-up issues.
